title: Helenium pinnatifidum
taxonomy: Helenium pinnatifidum is classified within the plant family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the order Asterales, which includes many flowering plants commonly known as composites or daisies. The family Asteraceae is further divided into multiple subfamilies, and Helenium pinnatifidum is typically assigned to the subfamily Asteroideae. This subfamily includes many flowering plants with composite flowers and is found in a wide range of habitats around the world. The classification of Helenium pinnatifidum is an example of how plants are grouped based on their evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics.

intro: Helenium pinnatifidum, also known as sneezeweed, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Helenium pinnatifidum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as helenalin and iso-helenalin, as well as flavonoids and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Helenium pinnatifidum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in folk medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
